QF.id: VIII Introduction to Clearing and Settlement System_46

question.text: Under physical settlement, a client with a long ITM call option position at expiry must:

answer.text: Pay the settlement amount and receive delivery of the underlying shares
Deliver the underlying shares to the clearing corporation
Receive cash equal to the intrinsic value of the option
Pay additional margin equal to the contract value

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** A long call option holder has the right to BUY the underlying. When exercised at expiry under physical settlement, the holder pays the strike price (settlement amount) and receives the underlying shares. The short call writer must deliver the shares.
**The Trap:** The long call buyer RECEIVES shares (does not deliver). The short call writer DELIVERS shares. For puts, the direction is reversed.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Understanding delivery obligations for each option position type is critical.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Long call at expiry: pay strike price, receive shares. Short call: deliver shares, receive strike price. Physical settlement only.
